name: From Mirador de Piedrasluengas to Potes and Fuente Dé — round trip
name_original: Desde el Mirador de Piedrasluengas a Potes y Fuente Dé — ida y vuelta
region_intro: Road cycling around Pesaguero, located in the Liébana region of Cantabria, Spain, offers varied terrain within the Picos de Europa and Cantabrian Mountains. The area features extensive forests of holm oaks, oaks, and beeches, alongside dramatic limestone spires and river valleys like the Río Buyón. Road cyclists can expect challenging climbs and well-maintained paved surfaces, providing a diverse landscape for exploration. Best road cycling routes around Pesaguero The most popular road cycling route is Potes to Fuente Dé Cycle Route, a 28.9 miles (46.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects two prominent locations with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is San Glorio to Collada de Llesba CIMA Climb, a difficult 35.2 miles (56.6 km) path. This route is known for its challenging ascents and panoramic mountain views. Local road cyclists also love the From Mirador de Piedrasluengas to Potes and Fuente Dé — round trip, a 63.7 miles (102.5 km) trail leading through the Picos de Europa and past the Mirador de Piedrasluengas, often completed in about 5 hours 41 minutes. Road cycling around Pesaguero is defined by dramatic mountain passes, dense forests, and scenic viewpoints over the Picos de Europa. The network offers options for difficult climbs and long-distance rides. The routes in Pesaguero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pesaguero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pesaguero?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Pesaguero, offering a variety of challenges from moderate to difficult. The region is known for its demanding climbs and scenic long-distance rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Pesaguero?
A: Road cycling in Pesaguero features varied terrain, from extensive forests of holm oaks, oaks, and beeches to dramatic limestone spires within the Picos de Europa and Cantabrian Mountains. You'll encounter significant elevation changes and stunning mountain passes.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Pesaguero?
A: While Pesaguero is primarily known for its challenging climbs, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. The majority of routes are classified as moderate or difficult, catering to cyclists looking for a significant challenge. For family-friendly options, consider routes with less elevation gain or explore the area's flatter sections if available.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling in Pesaguero?
A: Many cycling routes around Pesaguero are on mostly paved surfaces, suitable for road bikes. Despite the mountainous terrain, roads are often recently resurfaced and in excellent condition, even on the steepest climbs. Traffic density is generally low, allowing for a more peaceful cycling experience.

Q: Which routes offer the best panoramic views of the Picos de Europa?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that pass through high-altitude viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298665713/from-mirador-de-piedrasluengas-to-potes-and-fuente-de-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Mirador de Piedrasluengas to Potes and Fuente Dé — round trip&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the Mirador de Piedrasluengas, which offers spectacular vistas of the Picos de Europa, Peña Labra, and the Liébana and Valdeprado Valleys. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005126390/san-glorio-to-collada-de-llesba-cima-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Glorio to Collada de Llesba CIMA Climb&lt;/a&gt;, known for its panoramic mountain views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Pesaguero?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular road cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1298665713/from-mirador-de-piedrasluengas-to-potes-and-fuente-de-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Mirador de Piedrasluengas to Potes and Fuente Dé — round trip&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 102.5 km loop. Other notable circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14990383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Glorio Pass – Scenic Mountain Climb loop from Dobres&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corzo Viewpoint – San Glorio Pass loop from La Vega&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. Along your rides, you might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive mountain vistas. You could also pass by significant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4767542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcornocal de Tolibes&lt;/a&gt; forest. Charming villages with traditional Cantabrian architecture and rustic churches are also scattered throughout the territory.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about cycling in Pesaguero?
A: The road cycling routes around Pesaguero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, well-maintained roads, and the stunning, low-traffic scenery of the Picos de Europa.

Q: Is there parking available near the cycling routes in Pesaguero?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists are not detailed, Pesaguero and nearby villages like Potes offer various amenities. It's generally possible to find parking in or near these towns, which serve as common starting points for many routes. Always check local regulations for parking availability.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Pesaguero?
A: The Picos de Europa region, including Pesaguero, offers excellent cycling conditions during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons typically provide more stable weather, warmer temperatures, and clear views. However, be prepared for varying mountain weather, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs in the Pesaguero area that are comparable to famous European ascents?
A: Absolutely. The Pesaguero area and the wider Picos de Europa region are celebrated for their challenging climbs, often compared to those found in the Pyrenees and the Alps. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005126390/san-glorio-to-collada-de-llesba-cima-climb" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Glorio to Collada de Llesba CIMA Climb&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and are a true test for experienced road cyclists.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Pesaguero and the nearby larger town of Potes offer various amenities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. Potes, in particular, is a popular hub for visitors to the Liébana region and provides a good base with services for cyclists.
tips: Potes is one of the most beautiful towns in Cantabria and Spain. It has a privileged position to visit the Picos de Europa. It is near the point of arrival of the Camino Lebaniego and frankly, it is worth staying there at night to rest and take advantage of its restaurants and its small streets.

Camaleño is a town located in the westernmost part of Cantabria, just on the way to the famous Fuente Dé.
It is one of the seven municipalities in the Liébana region, in the Valdebaró Valley. Another charming place with spectacular views.

Nestled at the foot of the Picos de Europa, it is surrounded by spectacular mountains. The people of Espinama are dedicated to livestock and, above all, to the hotel business (taking advantage of the proximity to Fuente Dé and the presence of tourism). It is essential to visit the old church of the town, which is from the 17th century.

A lush and diverse environment with abundant species of birds, ibex and other animals. In addition, the area is perfect for taking photos. And of course, one of the main attractions of Fuente Dé is its cable car, which ascends visitors to an altitude of around 1,823 meters. The cable car ride is spectacular and offers panoramic views.
